command: mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway03.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user nC6sHo9eqXDn4Lp.root --database SPWUc63a3tjYuzCxiuEomB --execute SELECT * FROM business_plans LIMIT 1;
messages: 1	llc	Real-Eye-Nation LLC	NULL	1	To illuminate truth through authentic storytelling, empowering families and communities to document their histories, preserve their legacies, and share narratives that inspire generational transformation.	A world where every family has the tools and platform to tell their authentic story, where truth is documented and preserved for future generations, and where media serves as a vehicle for healing, education, and empowerment.	Real-Eye-Nation LLC operates as the media and content division within the LuvOnPurpose family of companies. The organization focuses on three primary areas: documentary content creation, narrative preservation services, and truth-based educational media. The company was established to address the gap in authentic family storytelling and community narrative preservation.	2024	[{"name":"Documentary Production","description":"Full-service documentary creation for families and organizations","target":"Families, nonprofits, community organizations"},{"name":"Legacy Documentation","description":"Oral history recording, family archive digitization, story preservation","target":"Multi-generational families"},{"name":"Media Consulting","description":"Strategy and guidance for authentic storytelling","target":"Small businesses, nonprofits"},{"name":"Educational Content","description":"Video courses, tutorials, and training materials","target":"Academy students, community members"},{"name":"Content Licensing","description":"Licensing of original content for educational and commercial use","target":"Educational institutions, media companies"},{"name":"Brand Storytelling","description":"Authentic brand narrative development for mission-driven organizations","target":"Social enterprises, nonprofits"}]	Integrated ecosystem with built-in distribution channels, purpose-driven approach prioritizing truth and empowerment, community trust through L.A.W.S. Collective connection, and blockchain-verified documentation for authenticity.	Primary: Multi-generational families seeking to document histories and preserve oral traditions. Secondary: Mission-driven nonprofits and social enterprises needing authentic storytelling. Tertiary: Educational institutions seeking culturally relevant content.	Growing genealogy market ($3B+), expanding documentary film market, increasing demand for authentic organizational storytelling	Operating within LuvOnPurpose ecosystem provides built-in distribution, educational partnerships, and community access. Purpose-driven approach prioritizes truth and healing over entertainment value.	4	Lean team structure leveraging broader LuvOnPurpose family for support functions	[{"bio": "Vision, strategy, and creative oversight", "name": "LaShanna Russell", "role": "Founder/Creative Director"}, {"bio": "Content creation and editing (training in progress)", "name": "Family Members", "role": "Media Production Support"}, {"bio": "Coordination with 508 Academy, Education/Training Manager", "name": "Cornelius", "role": "Education Liaison"}]	20000.00	2500.00	17500.00	75000.00	200000.00	18-24 months	20000.00	Real-Eye-Nation LLC addresses a critical gap in authentic media representation and cultural preservation for underserved communities across the United States. According to recent studies, less than 5% of media content accurately represents the histories, narratives, and lived experiences of Black and Indigenous communities, resulting in widespread cultural erasure and the loss of ancestral knowledge that has sustained families for generations. This systematic underrepresentation has profound consequences: youth disconnected from their heritage, families unable to document and preserve their legacies, and communities whose stories remain untold or are told by others without authentic understanding.

The root causes of this crisis are systemic. Traditional media production requires significant capital investment—equipment, studio space, distribution networks—that has historically been inaccessible to community-based organizations. Furthermore, mainstream media platforms prioritize content that serves dominant cultural narratives, leaving community stories marginalized or commodified. The result is a generation at risk of losing connection to the wisdom, practices, and histories that define their identity.

Currently, few organizations exist that combine professional media production capabilities with deep community roots and cultural competency. Those that do often lack the resources to scale their impact or sustain operations. Real-Eye-Nation is uniquely positioned to fill this void. Founded by community members with both professional media credentials and lived experience, we bring authentic voice to documentary production, educational content creation, and digital archiving.

Our organization requires $75,000 to $150,000 in grant funding to expand our media production infrastructure and community outreach capacity. Specifically, these funds will enable us to: (1) upgrade our production equipment to broadcast-quality standards, allowing us to create content suitable for major streaming platforms and educational institutions; (2) hire two additional content creators from the communities we serve, providing living-wage employment while ensuring authentic representation; (3) develop our digital archive platform to preserve and make accessible over 500 hours of community oral histories and cultural documentation; and (4) launch our "Truth Tellers" youth program, training 50 young people annually in media production skills while documenting their family histories.

Within 18 months of receiving funding, we project the following measurable outcomes: production of 12 documentary short films highlighting community histories and contemporary stories; creation of a 40-episode educational podcast series reaching an estimated 25,000 listeners; training of 50 youth in professional media production; preservation of 200 family oral histories in our digital archive; and establishment of partnerships with 5 educational institutions for curriculum integration. These outcomes directly serve our target population of 10,000 community members in the greater Atlanta metropolitan area, with digital content reaching national and international audiences.

The sustainability of this initiative extends beyond the grant period through multiple revenue streams we are developing: licensing fees from educational institutions, streaming platform distribution agreements, and fee-for-service family documentation packages. Our business model projects break-even operations within 24 months, with grant funding providing the essential bridge to self-sufficiency. By investing in Real-Eye-Nation, funders invest not merely in a media company, but in the preservation of cultural heritage and the empowerment of communities to tell their own stories for generations to come.	[{"amount": 10000, "source": "Small Business Grants (Women-owned)", "status": "Seeking"}, {"amount": 10000, "source": "Small Business Grants (Minority-owned)", "status": "Seeking"}, {"amount": 5000, "source": "Foundation Grants (Media/Storytelling)", "status": "Seeking"}]	["Equipment acquisition and setup", "Complete production training", "Launch pilot documentary project", "Build initial content library"]	["Establish sustainable revenue from content licensing", "Expand team with trained family members", "Create distribution partnerships", "Build library of 100+ community narratives"]	[{"milestone": "Equipment Acquisition", "status": "pending", "targetDate": "Month 3"}, {"milestone": "First Pilot Project", "status": "pending", "targetDate": "Month 6"}, {"milestone": "First Paying Client", "status": "pending", "targetDate": "Month 9"}, {"milestone": "Content Library (10 pieces)", "status": "pending", "targetDate": "Month 12"}]	Cultural preservation of family histories, community empowerment through storytelling tools, educational access with culturally relevant content, truth documentation via blockchain verification, healing through narrative processes.	Training community members in media production, providing free/reduced-cost services to families in need, creating employment opportunities, building accessible community narrative library.	completed	NULL	NULL	NULL	1	NULL	2026-01-17 03:55:42	2026-01-19 03:41:29	NULL
